definition: Attention directed in a way that feeds confusion, reactivity, and unskillful patterns.
notes: The project prefers unwise attention because it pairs transparently with yoniso manasikāra and stays close to the operative function of the term. Misdirected attention can work when the English sentence wants to stress error in direction rather than lack of intelligence. In this repository, ayoniso manasikāra is the negative side of the opening MN 2 contrast: it is the mode of attending through which unarisen outflows arise and arisen outflows grow. SN 46.51 then extends the same pattern into repeated formulas where frequent unwise attention feeds the hindrances and starves the awakening factors. Keep the term as active path-negative attention, not as a comment about stupidity or an abstract irrationality slogan. Ven. Dr. M. Punnaji's attention-centered model reinforces this entry as the distorted counterpart to wise inwardly clarifying attention.

context_rules.context: In doctrinal contrast with yoniso manasikāra
In explanatory prose emphasizing deviation or poor direction
context_rules.notes: Allow this as a controlled alternate only when the context clearly supports it.

context_rules.context: When the passage emphasizes the sort of attending that feeds confusion and distractions, including the repeated bahulīkāra formulas
context_rules.notes: Keep the path-negative function of the term explicit and do not recast it as an abstract irrationality label.

example_phrases.notes: Shows a key phrase in the analysis of what should and should not be attended to.
Shows the feeding-side formula used with hindrances and the starving-side formula used with awakening factors.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the term's role in feeding the arising and growth of outflows at the opening of the āsava sequence.
Supports the repeated feeding and starving formulas built around unwise attention.
Named in notes as support for the current ayoniso manasikāra policy.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'unwise attention' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use 'misdirected attention' only when local prose clearly benefits. Do not rotate into carelessness-language or irrational-application language without an explicitly documented reason.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward carelessness-language, irrational-application language, or other unrecorded alternates inside the wise/unwise attention pair.
